summary: TOOL-GRAPH plan: TOOL_CATEGORIES deletion removes the only fallback — reversibility is fragile
detail: The plan deletes TOOL_CATEGORIES (typed-result.ts:225-233) and toolCategory() (typed-result.ts:235-243) without preserving them as a fallback. If the graph doesn't load or has bugs, the system has no alternative suggestion mechanism. The plan should keep TOOL_CATEGORIES as a fallback and only use graph suggestions when available.

summary: TOOL-GRAPH plan: No error clarity for unknown tool IDs, missing graph, or unset graph
detail: The plan doesn't specify behavior for: (1) findDownstream("unknown_id") — empty array? diagnostic fallback? error? (2) setToolGraph() never called — does the module crash or silently return wrong suggestions? (3) suggestNext() returning Effect — succeed([]) or fail()? No Effect.withSpan or Effect.annotateCurrentSpan anywhere in the plan design.

summary: TOOL-GRAPH plan: 14 categories vs terrain map's 17 — unexplained discrepancy
detail: The architecture plan claims 14 categories but the terrain map (build/rig-relay/cartography/tool-graph-terrain-map.v1.json) proposed 17 categories in its graph schema design. The plan doesn't explain which 3 categories were dropped or why 14 is the right number. The jump from 7 to 14 needs justification.

summary: TOOL-GRAPH plan overlaps with tool-affordances plan without cross-reference
detail: Both plans modify the same functions in typed-result.ts (suggestedToolsAfterSuccess, toolCategory). The tool-affordances plan extends toolCategory with per-tool-ID branches; TOOL-GRAPH deletes it entirely. If tool-affordances executes first, TOOL-GRAPH must undo its changes. TOOL-GRAPH should acknowledge and declare itself as a structural superset.

summary: TOOL-GRAPH: 10 planned test cases insufficient — minimum 17-24 needed
detail: The plan's 10 test cases cannot cover the full change. Minimum 17-24 needed: 6-8 for ToolGraph class, 3-4 for buildGraph(), 4-6 for setToolGraph() injection, 2-3 for suggestNext(), and 2-3 for TOOL_CATEGORIES deletion regression. The setToolGraph() injection seam requires Effect test infrastructure (~40 lines) and cannot be verified with a bun -e script.

summary: TOOL-GRAPH: No edge-to-node validation — orphan edges are silent data bugs
detail: Exhibit-a agent confirmed: buildGraph() has zero edge-to-node validation. If a tool is renamed or removed, any seeded edge referencing that tool ID becomes orphaned. findDownstream() returns suggestions for nonexistent tools — the LLM receives dead suggestions. No compile-time or runtime validation. Every tool rename is a potential silent data corruption bug. Fix: filter orphan edges in buildGraph() and emit structured log warning.
